<content>
1: // ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
2: // ║ lib/rollback                                                             ║
3: // ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
4: // ║ Round rollback: move failed round artifacts and append supersede events  ║
5: // ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
6: 
7: 'use strict';
8: 
9: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
10: // 1. IMPORTS
11: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
12: 
13: const fs = require('node:fs');
14: const path = require('node:path');
15: const { appendJsonlEvent, normalizeRoundId } = require('./audit-trail.cjs');
16: 
17: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
18: // 2. CONSTANTS
19: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
20: 
21: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
22: // 3. HELPERS
23: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
24: 
25: function safeTimestamp(timestamp) {
26:   return String(timestamp || new Date().toISOString()).replace(/[:.]/g, '-');
27: }
28: 
29: function moveIfExists(source, target) {
30:   if (!fs.existsSync(source)) return null;
31:   fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(target), { recursive: true });
32:   fs.rmSync(target, { recursive: true, force: true });
33:   fs.renameSync(source, target);
34:   return target;
35: }
36: 
37: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
38: // 4. CORE LOGIC
39: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
40: 
41: /**
42:  * Move the selected round artifacts into a failed-round archive folder.
43:  *
44:  * @param {string} packetSpecFolder - Packet spec folder containing ai-council
45:  * @param {string|number} roundId - Round id or integer round number
46:  * @param {Object} [options={}] - Rollback options
47:  * @param {string} [options.timestamp] - Timestamp used in the failed folder
48:  * @returns {Object} Failed archive root and moved artifact paths
49:  */
50: function moveRoundToFailed(packetSpecFolder, roundId, options = {}) {
51:   const round = normalizeRoundId(roundId);
52:   const councilRoot = path.resolve(packetSpecFolder, 'ai-council');
53:   const failedRoot = path.join(councilRoot, 'failed', `${round}-${safeTimestamp(options.timestamp)}`);
54:   const moved = [];
55: 
56:   const candidates = [
57:     [path.join(councilRoot, round), path.join(failedRoot, round)],
58:     [path.join(councilRoot, 'seats', round), path.join(failedRoot, 'seats', round)],
59:     [path.join(councilRoot, 'deliberations', `${round}.md`), path.join(failedRoot, 'deliberations', `${round}.md`)],
60:     [path.join(councilRoot, 'critiques', `${round}-critique.md`), path.join(failedRoot, 'critiques', `${round}-critique.md`)],
61:   ];
62: 
63:   for (const [source, target] of candidates) {
64:     const movedPath = moveIfExists(source, target);
65:     if (movedPath) moved.push(movedPath);
66:   }
67: 
68:   return { round_id: round, failedRoot, moved };
69: }
70: 
71: function readEvents(stateJsonlPath) {
72:   if (!fs.existsSync(stateJsonlPath)) return [];
73:   return fs.readFileSync(stateJsonlPath, 'utf8')
74:     .split(/\r?\n/)
75:     .map((line) => line.trim())
76:     .filter(Boolean)
77:     .map((line) => JSON.parse(line));
78: }
79: 
80: /**
81:  * Append rollback and artifact_superseded markers for a round.
82:  *
83:  * @param {string} stateJsonlPath - Path to the state JSONL file
84:  * @param {Object} [options={}] - Supersede options
85:  * @param {string|number} [options.round_id] - Round id to mark superseded
86:  * @param {string|number} [options.roundId] - Camel-case round id alias
87:  * @param {string} [options.timestamp] - Event timestamp
88:  * @param {string} [options.rollback_event_id] - Rollback event id
89:  * @param {string} [options.rollbackEventId] - Camel-case rollback id alias
90:  * @param {string} [options.reason] - Rollback reason
91:  * @returns {Object} Superseded artifact summary
92:  */
93: function markSuperseded(stateJsonlPath, options = {}) {
94:   const round = normalizeRoundId(options.round_id || options.roundId || 1);
95:   const timestamp = options.timestamp || new Date().toISOString();
96:   const rollbackEventId = options.rollback_event_id || options.rollbackEventId || `rollback-${round}-${Date.now()}`;
97:   const reason = options.reason || 'round rollback';
98:   const events = readEvents(stateJsonlPath);
99:   const priorMarkers = new Set(
100:     events
101:       .filter((event) => event.event === 'artifact_superseded' && event.round_id === round)
102:       .map((event) => event.original_path),
103:   );
104:   const artifactEvents = events.filter(
105:     (event) => event.event === 'artifact_written' && event.round_id === round && !priorMarkers.has(event.path),
106:   );
107: 
108:   appendJsonlEvent(stateJsonlPath, {
109:     event: 'rollback',
110:     round_id: round,
111:     reason,
112:     timestamp,
113:     rollback_event_id: rollbackEventId,
114:     supersedes: artifactEvents.map((event) => event.event_id || event.path),
115:   });
116: 
117:   const appended = [];
118:   for (const artifact of artifactEvents) {
119:     appendJsonlEvent(stateJsonlPath, {
120:       event: 'artifact_superseded',
121:       original_path: artifact.path,
122:       round_id: round,
123:       rollback_event_id: rollbackEventId,
124:       superseded_by: 'rollback',
125:       timestamp,
126:     });
127:     appended.push(artifact.path);
128:   }
129: 
130:   return { round_id: round, rollback_event_id: rollbackEventId, superseded: appended };
131: }
132: 
133: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
134: // 5. EXPORTS
135: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
136: 
137: module.exports = {
138:   moveRoundToFailed,
139:   markSuperseded,
140: };

(End of file - total 140 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>
1: // ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
2: // ║ lib/persist-artifacts (library)                                          ║
3: // ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
4: // ║ Council artifact writers, parser, and renderer with scoped writes        ║
5: // ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
6: 
7: 'use strict';
8: 
9: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
10: // 1. IMPORTS
11: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
12: 
13: const fs = require('node:fs');
14: const path = require('node:path');
15: const {
16:   SCHEMA_VERSION,
17:   PROTOCOL,
18:   PRODUCER_VERSION,
19:   appendArtifactWrittenEvent,
20:   computeChecksum,
21:   normalizeEvent,
22:   normalizeRoundId,
23: } = require('./audit-trail.cjs');
24: 
25: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
26: // 2. CONSTANTS
27: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
28: 
29: const DEFAULT_TIMESTAMP = '1970-01-01T00:00:00.000Z';
30: const DEFAULT_MAX_ROUNDS = 3;
31: const MAX_SLUG_LENGTH = 80;
32: const OPTIONAL_ALIASES = {
33:   crossReferences: ['cross-references', 'cross references'],
34:   droppedAlternatives: ['dropped alternatives'],
35:   deliberationNotes: ['deliberation notes', 'deliberation notes details'],
36:   risksMitigations: ['risks & mitigations', 'risks and mitigations', 'risks & mitigations details'],
37: };
38: 
39: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
40: // 3. HELPERS
41: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
42: 
43: function normalizeText(value) {
44:   return String(value || '').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
45: }
46: 
47: function normalizeHeading(value) {
48:   return normalizeText(value)
49:     .toLowerCase()
50:     .replace(/^#+\s*/, '')
51:     .replace(/^§?\d+[\).\s-]+/, '')
52:     .replace(/[`:]+$/g, '')
53:     .trim();
54: }
55: 
56: function slugify(value) {
57:   return normalizeText(value)
58:     .toLowerCase()
59:     .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-')
60:     .replace(/^-+|-+$/g, '')
61:     .slice(0, MAX_SLUG_LENGTH) || 'unknown';
62: }
63: 
64: function metadataEvent(event) {
65:   const { schema_version, protocol, producer, ...payload } = event || {};
66:   return normalizeEvent(payload);
67: }
68: 
69: /**
70:  * Parse one JSONL state event and preserve legacy schema defaults.
71:  *
72:  * @param {string} line - JSONL line to parse
73:  * @returns {Object} Parsed state event with schema metadata
74:  * @throws {Error} When the state line is not a JSON object
75:  */
76: function parseStateLine(line) {
77:   const event = JSON.parse(line);
78:   if (typeof event !== 'object' || event === null || Array.isArray(event)) {
79:     throw new Error('[ai-council] Expected object state event');
80:   }
81:   return {
82:     schema_version: '1',
83:     ...event,
84:   };
85: }
86: 
87: /**
88:  * Parse a council JSONL state log into normalized event objects.
89:  *
90:  * @param {string} jsonl - State log content
91:  * @returns {Object[]} Parsed state events
92:  */
93: function parseStateLog(jsonl) {
94:   return String(jsonl || '')
95:     .split(/\r?\n/)
96:     .map((line) => line.trim())
97:     .filter(Boolean)
98:     .map(parseStateLine);
99: }
100: 
101: function roundLabel(round) {
102:   return normalizeRoundId(round);
103: }
104: 
105: function splitSections(markdown) {
106:   const source = String(markdown || '').replace(/\r\n/g, '\n');
107:   const headingPattern = /^(#{2,6})\s+(.+)$/gm;
108:   const matches = [...source.matchAll(headingPattern)];
109:   return matches.map((match, index) => {
110:     const start = match.index + match[0].length;
111:     const end = index + 1 < matches.length ? matches[index + 1].index : source.length;
112:     return {
113:       level: match[1].length,
114:       heading: normalizeText(match[2]),
115:       normalized: normalizeHeading(match[2]),
116:       content: source.slice(start, end).trim(),
117:       raw: source.slice(match.index, end).trim(),
118:     };
119:   });
120: }
121: 
122: function findSection(sections, aliases) {
123:   const normalizedAliases = aliases.map(normalizeHeading);
124:   return sections.find((section) => normalizedAliases.includes(section.normalized)) || null;
125: }
126: 
127: function parseMarkdownTable(sectionText) {
128:   const lines = String(sectionText || '')
129:     .split('\n')
130:     .map((line) => line.trim())
131:     .filter((line) => line.startsWith('|') && line.endsWith('|'));
132:   if (lines.length < 3) return [];
133: 
134:   const headers = lines[0].split('|').slice(1, -1).map(normalizeHeading);
135:   return lines.slice(2)
136:     .filter((line) => !/^\|?\s*:?-{3,}:?\s*(\|\s*:?-{3,}:?\s*)+\|?$/.test(line))
137:     .map((line) => {
138:       const cells = line.split('|').slice(1, -1).map((cell) => normalizeText(cell));
139:       return headers.reduce((record, header, index) => {
140:         record[header] = cells[index] || '';
141:         return record;
142:       }, {});
143:     })
144:     .filter((record) => Object.values(record).some(Boolean));
145: }
146: 
147: function normalizeSeatId(value, index) {
148:   const text = normalizeText(value);
149:   const numberMatch = text.match(/(?:seat[-\s]*)?(\d{1,3})/i);
150:   const seatNumber = numberMatch ? Number(numberMatch[1]) : index + 1;
151:   return `seat-${String(seatNumber).padStart(3, '0')}`;
152: }
153: 
154: function parseCompositionSeats(composition) {
155:   return parseMarkdownTable(composition ? composition.content : '').map((row, index) => {
156:     const seatLabel = row.seat || `seat-${String(index + 1).padStart(3, '0')}`;
157:     return {
158:       id: normalizeSeatId(seatLabel, index),
159:       label: seatLabel,
160:       lens: row['strategy lens'] || row.lens || 'Unknown',
161:       vantage: row['ai vantage target'] || row.vantage || 'Unknown',
162:       mandate: row['distinct mandate'] || row.mandate || '',
163:       confidence: row.confidence || '',
164:       content: '',
165:       source: 'composition-table',
166:     };
167:   });
168: }
169: 
170: function parseSeatSections(sections, compositionSeats) {
171:   const seatSections = sections.filter((section) => /^seat\s+\d{1,3}\b|^seat-\d{1,3}\b/i.test(section.normalized));
172:   if (!seatSections.length) return compositionSeats;
173: 
174:   return seatSections.map((section, index) => {
175:     const fallback = compositionSeats[index] || {};
176:     const id = normalizeSeatId(section.heading, index);
177:     const title = section.heading.replace(/^Seat\s+\d+\s*[-:—]\s*/i, '');
178:     const parts = title.split('/').map(normalizeText);
179:     return {
180:       id,
181:       label: id,
182:       lens: parts[0] || fallback.lens || 'Unknown',
183:       vantage: parts[1] || fallback.vantage || 'Unknown',
184:       mandate: fallback.mandate || '',
185:       confidence: fallback.confidence || '',
186:       content: section.content,
187:       source: 'seat-heading',
188:     };
189:   });
190: }
191: 
192: function parsePlanConfidence(section) {
193:   if (!section) return null;
194:   const overall = section.content.match(/\*\*Overall\*\*\s*:\s*([0-9]{1,3})%?/i)
195:     || section.content.match(/Overall\s*:\s*([0-9]{1,3})%?/i)
196:     || section.content.match(/([0-9]{1,3})\s*\/\s*100/);
197:   return overall ? Number(overall[1]) : null;
198: }
199: 
200: function collectOptionalSections(sections) {
201:   return Object.entries(OPTIONAL_ALIASES).reduce((result, [key, aliases]) => {
202:     const section = findSection(sections, aliases);
203:     result[key] = section ? section.content : '';
204:     return result;
205:   }, {});
206: }
207: 
208: function collectExtraSections(sections) {
209:   const known = new Set([
210:     'task classification',
211:     'council composition',
212:     'strategy comparison',
213:     'deliberation notes',
214:     'winning strategy',
215:     'recommended plan',
216:     'implementation steps',
217:     'prerequisites',
218:     'plan confidence',
219:     'dropped alternatives',
220:     'risks & mitigations',
221:     'risks and mitigations',
222:     'planning-only boundary',
223:     'cross-references',
224:   ]);
225:   return sections
226:     .filter((section) => !known.has(section.normalized) && !/^seat\s+\d{1,3}\b|^seat-\d{1,3}\b/i.test(section.normalized))
227:     .map((section) => section.heading);
228: }
229: 
230: /**
231:  * Throw OUT_OF_SCOPE_WRITE if a relative path attempts parent-directory traversal.
232:  *
233:  * @param {string} relativePath - Caller-provided relative path
234:  * @param {string} aiCouncilRoot - Absolute path of the council root
235:  * @throws {Error} With code 'OUT_OF_SCOPE_WRITE' when traversal is detected
236:  */
237: function rejectParentTraversal(relativePath, aiCouncilRoot) {
238:   if (String(relativePath).split(/[\\/]+/).includes('..')) {
239:     const error = new Error(`OUT_OF_SCOPE_WRITE: Refusing to write outside ${aiCouncilRoot}: ${relativePath}`);
240:     error.code = 'OUT_OF_SCOPE_WRITE';
241:     throw error;
242:   }
243: }
244: 
245: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
246: // 4. CORE LOGIC
247: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
248: 
249: /**
250:  * Parse a council report into the artifact writer's structured model.
251:  *
252:  * @param {string} markdown - Council report markdown
253:  * @returns {Object} Parsed council report with validation details
254:  */
255: function parseCouncilReport(markdown) {
256:   const sectionsList = splitSections(markdown);
257:   const title = String(markdown || '').replace(/\r\n/g, '\n').split('\n').find((line) => /^#{1,2}\s+/.test(line));
258:   const composition = findSection(sectionsList, ['Council Composition']);
259:   const recommendedPlan = findSection(sectionsList, ['Recommended Plan']);
260:   const planConfidence = findSection(sectionsList, ['Plan Confidence']);
261:   const compositionSeats = parseCompositionSeats(composition);
262:   const seats = parseSeatSections(sectionsList, compositionSeats);
263: 
264:   const missing = [];
265:   if (!composition) missing.push('Council Composition');
266:   if (!seats.length) missing.push('Per-seat sections');
267:   if (!recommendedPlan) missing.push('Recommended Plan');
268:   if (!planConfidence) missing.push('Plan Confidence');
269: 
270:   return {
271:     ok: missing.length === 0,
272:     missing,
273:     title: title ? normalizeText(title.replace(/^#{1,2}\s+/, '')) : '',
274:     sections: sectionsList.reduce((result, section) => {
275:       result[section.heading] = section.content;
276:       return result;
277:     }, {}),
278:     seats,
279:     composition: composition ? composition.content : '',
280:     recommendedPlan: recommendedPlan ? recommendedPlan.content : '',
281:     planConfidence: parsePlanConfidence(planConfidence),
282:     optionalSections: collectOptionalSections(sectionsList),
283:     extraSections: collectExtraSections(sectionsList),
284:     rawMarkdown: String(markdown || '').replace(/\r\n/g, '\n').trim(),
285:   };
286: }
287: 
288: function plainText(markdown) {
289:   return normalizeText(String(markdown || '')
290:     .replace(/`([^`]+)`/g, '$1')
291:     .replace(/\*\*([^*]+)\*\*/g, '$1')
292:     .replace(/\[([^\]]+)\]\([^)]+\)/g, '$1')
293:     .replace(/^#{1,6}\s+/gm, '')
294:     .replace(/^\s*[-*]\s+/gm, '')
295:     .replace(/^\s*\d+\.\s+/gm, ''));
296: }
297: 
298: function firstParagraph(markdown) {
299:   const blocks = String(markdown || '')
300:     .split(/\n\s*\n/)
301:     .map(plainText)
302:     .filter(Boolean);
303:   return blocks[0] || 'Multi-AI Council completed and produced a recommended plan.';
304: }
305: 
306: function listItems(markdown) {
307:   const items = [];
308:   for (const line of String(markdown || '').split(/\r?\n/)) {
309:     const match = line.match(/^\s*(?:[-*]|\d+\.)\s+(.+)$/);
310:     if (match) items.push(plainText(match[1]));
311:   }
312:   return items.filter(Boolean);
313: }
314: 
315: /**
316:  * Build a memory-save payload from a parsed council report.
317:  *
318:  * @param {Object} parsed - Parsed council report
319:  * @param {string} packetSpecFolder - Packet spec folder for the payload
320:  * @returns {Object} Memory-save payload
321:  */
322: function buildMemorySavePayload(parsed, packetSpecFolder) {
323:   const recommendedPlan = parsed.recommendedPlan || '';
324:   const droppedAlternatives = parsed.optionalSections.droppedAlternatives || '';
325:   const risksMitigations = parsed.optionalSections.risksMitigations || '';
326:   const implementationSteps = parsed.sections['Implementation Steps'] || '';
327:   const decisions = listItems(recommendedPlan).concat(listItems(droppedAlternatives));
328:   const followUps = listItems(risksMitigations).concat(listItems(implementationSteps));
329: 
330:   return {
331:     topic: parsed.title || 'Multi-AI Council',
332:     spec_folder: path.resolve(packetSpecFolder),
333:     session_summary: firstParagraph(recommendedPlan),
334:     decisions,
335:     files_changed: [],
336:     follow_ups: followUps,
337:     tests: [],
338:     completion_status: followUps.length ? 'complete-with-deferrals' : 'complete',
339:   };
340: }
341: 
342: function renderSeatArtifact(seat, round) {
343:   return `---
344: round: ${round}
345: seat: ${seat.id}
346: executor: ${slugify(seat.vantage)}
347: lens: ${JSON.stringify(seat.lens)}
348: status: ok
349: timestamp: ${DEFAULT_TIMESTAMP}
350: simulated: false
351: ---
352: 
353: # ${seat.id}: ${seat.lens} / ${seat.vantage}
354: 
355: ## Mandate
356: ${seat.mandate || '[No mandate captured]'}
357: 
358: ## Confidence
359: ${seat.confidence || '[No confidence captured]'}
360: 
361: ## Seat Output
362: ${seat.content || '[No per-seat section was present; derived from Council Composition table]'}
363: `;
364: }
365: 
366: function optionalBlock(title, value, strictOutput) {
367:   if (value) return `## ${title}\n${value}\n`;
368:   return strictOutput ? '' : `## ${title}\n[Optional section not present in source report]\n`;
369: }
370: 
371: /**
372:  * Render parsed council data into all persisted artifact payloads.
373:  *
374:  * @param {Object} parsed - Parsed council report
375:  * @param {Object} [options={}] - Rendering options
376:  * @param {number} [options.round=1] - Council round number
377:  * @param {boolean} [options.strictOutput=false] - Omit missing optional blocks
378:  * @param {string} [options.timestamp] - Artifact timestamp
379:  * @param {string} [options.specFolder] - Packet spec folder path
380:  * @param {number} [options.maxRounds] - Configured maximum round count
381:  * @returns {Object} Rendered artifact payloads and relative paths
382:  */
383: function renderArtifacts(parsed, options = {}) {
384:   const round = Number(options.round || 1);
385:   const roundDir = roundLabel(round);
386:   const strictOutput = Boolean(options.strictOutput);
387:   const timestamp = options.timestamp || DEFAULT_TIMESTAMP;
388:   const specFolder = options.specFolder || '<packet-spec-folder>';
389: 
390:   const config = `${JSON.stringify({
391:     spec_folder: specFolder,
392:     current_round: round,
393:     max_rounds: options.maxRounds || DEFAULT_MAX_ROUNDS,
394:     seats_per_round: parsed.seats.length,
395:     convergence_signal: 'two-of-three-agree',
396:     created_at: timestamp,
397:     updated_at: timestamp,
398:     status: 'complete',
399:   }, null, 2)}\n`;
400: 
401:   const strategy = `# Multi-AI Council Strategy
402: 
403: ## Purpose
404: Persist a planning-only council report into packet-local ai-council artifacts.
405: 
406: ## Composition
407: ${parsed.composition || '[Missing composition]'}
408: 
409: ## Recommended Plan
410: ${parsed.recommendedPlan || '[Missing recommended plan]'}
411: `;
412: 
413:   const seats = parsed.seats.map((seat, index) => ({
414:     path: `seats/${roundDir}/${seat.id}-${slugify(seat.vantage || `seat-${index + 1}`)}.md`,
415:     content: renderSeatArtifact(seat, round),
416:   }));
417: 
418:   const deliberation = `# Multi-AI Council Deliberation ${roundDir}
419: 
420: ## Council Composition
421: ${parsed.composition}
422: 
423: ${optionalBlock('Deliberation Notes', parsed.optionalSections.deliberationNotes, strictOutput)}
424: ${optionalBlock('Risks & Mitigations', parsed.optionalSections.risksMitigations, strictOutput)}
425: ## Recommended Plan
426: ${parsed.recommendedPlan}
427: 
428: ## Plan Confidence
429: ${parsed.planConfidence === null ? '[No numeric confidence captured]' : `${parsed.planConfidence}/100`}
430: `;
431: 
432:   const stateEvents = [
433:     { event: 'round_start', round, timestamp, seats: parsed.seats.map((seat) => seat.id) },
434:     ...parsed.seats.map((seat) => ({ event: 'seat_returned', round, seat: seat.id, timestamp, status: 'ok' })),
435:     { event: 'deliberation_synthesized', round, timestamp, convergence_score: parsed.planConfidence },
436:     { event: 'round_end', round, timestamp, new_findings_count: parsed.extraSections.length },
437:     { event: 'council_complete', timestamp, final_report_path: 'ai-council/council-report.md', convergence: true },
438:   ].map(metadataEvent);
439: 
440:   const stateLog = `${stateEvents.map((event) => JSON.stringify(event)).join('\n')}\n`;
441:   const councilReport = `${parsed.rawMarkdown}\n`;
442:   return { config, strategy, stateLog, seats, deliberation, deliberationPath: `deliberations/${roundDir}.md`, councilReport };
443: }
444: 
445: function assertInside(baseDir, targetPath) {
446:   const relative = path.relative(baseDir, targetPath);
447:   if (relative.startsWith('..') || path.isAbsolute(relative)) {
448:     const error = new Error(`OUT_OF_SCOPE_WRITE: Refusing to write outside ${baseDir}: ${targetPath}`);
449:     error.code = 'OUT_OF_SCOPE_WRITE';
450:     throw error;
451:   }
452: }
453: 
454: function statePathFor(aiCouncilRoot) {
455:   return path.resolve(aiCouncilRoot, 'ai-council-state.jsonl');
456: }
457: 
458: function writeFileScoped(aiCouncilRoot, relativePath, content, options = {}) {
459:   const targetPath = path.resolve(aiCouncilRoot, relativePath);
460:   assertInside(aiCouncilRoot, targetPath);
461:   fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(targetPath), { recursive: true });
462:   fs.writeFileSync(targetPath, content, 'utf8');
463:   if (options.audit !== false) {
464:     const writtenContent = fs.readFileSync(targetPath);
465:     appendArtifactWrittenEvent(statePathFor(aiCouncilRoot), {
466:       path: relativePath.replace(/\\/g, '/'),
467:       bytes: writtenContent.length,
468:       checksum: computeChecksum(writtenContent),
469:       timestamp: options.timestamp || new Date().toISOString(),
470:       seat_id: options.seat_id || options.seatId || null,
471:       round_id: options.round_id || options.roundId || 1,
472:       maxBytes: options.maxBytes,
473:     });
474:   }
475:   return targetPath;
476: }
477: 
478: function councilRootFor(packetSpecFolder) {
479:   if (!packetSpecFolder) throw new Error('[ai-council] packet spec folder is required');
480:   const packetRoot = path.resolve(packetSpecFolder);
481:   const aiCouncilRoot = path.resolve(packetRoot, 'ai-council');
482:   assertInside(packetRoot, aiCouncilRoot);
483:   return { packetRoot, aiCouncilRoot };
484: }
485: 
486: /**
487:  * Write the council configuration artifact.
488:  *
489:  * @param {string} packetSpecFolder - Packet spec folder to write into
490:  * @param {string} content - Configuration JSON content
491:  * @param {Object} [options={}] - Scoped write and audit options
492:  * @returns {string} Absolute path to the written artifact
493:  */
494: function writeConfig(packetSpecFolder, content, options = {}) {
495:   const { aiCouncilRoot } = councilRootFor(packetSpecFolder);
496:   fs.mkdirSync(aiCouncilRoot, { recursive: true });
497:   return writeFileScoped(aiCouncilRoot, 'ai-council-config.json', content, options);
498: }
499: 
500: /**
501:  * Write the council strategy markdown artifact.
502:  *
503:  * @param {string} packetSpecFolder - Packet spec folder to write into
504:  * @param {string} content - Strategy markdown content
505:  * @param {Object} [options={}] - Scoped write and audit options
506:  * @returns {string} Absolute path to the written artifact
507:  */
508: function writeStrategyMd(packetSpecFolder, content, options = {}) {
509:   const { aiCouncilRoot } = councilRootFor(packetSpecFolder);
510:   fs.mkdirSync(aiCouncilRoot, { recursive: true });
511:   return writeFileScoped(aiCouncilRoot, 'ai-council-strategy.md', content, options);
512: }
513: 
514: /**
515:  * Write the council state JSONL artifact.
516:  *
517:  * @param {string} packetSpecFolder - Packet spec folder to write into
518:  * @param {string} content - State JSONL content
519:  * @param {Object} [options={}] - Scoped write and audit options
520:  * @returns {string} Absolute path to the written artifact
521:  */
522: function writeStateJsonl(packetSpecFolder, content, options = {}) {
523:   const { aiCouncilRoot } = councilRootFor(packetSpecFolder);
524:   fs.mkdirSync(aiCouncilRoot, { recursive: true });
525:   return writeFileScoped(aiCouncilRoot, 'ai-council-state.jsonl', content, options);
526: }
527: 
528: /**
529:  * Write a seat artifact under the council seats directory.
530:  *
531:  * @param {string} packetSpecFolder - Packet spec folder to write into
532:  * @param {string} relativePath - Seat-relative or ai-council-relative path
533:  * @param {string} content - Seat markdown content
534:  * @param {Object} [options={}] - Scoped write and audit options
535:  * @returns {string} Absolute path to the written artifact
536:  */
537: function writeSeat(packetSpecFolder, relativePath, content, options = {}) {
538:   const { aiCouncilRoot } = councilRootFor(packetSpecFolder);
539:   fs.mkdirSync(aiCouncilRoot, { recursive: true });
540:   rejectParentTraversal(relativePath, aiCouncilRoot);
541:   const seatPath = relativePath.startsWith('seats/') ? relativePath : path.join('seats', relativePath);
542:   return writeFileScoped(aiCouncilRoot, seatPath, content, options);
543: }
544: 
545: /**
546:  * Write a deliberation artifact under the council deliberations directory.
547:  *
548:  * @param {string} packetSpecFolder - Packet spec folder to write into
549:  * @param {string} relativePath - Deliberation-relative or ai-council-relative path
550:  * @param {string} content - Deliberation markdown content
551:  * @param {Object} [options={}] - Scoped write and audit options
552:  * @returns {string} Absolute path to the written artifact
553:  */
554: function writeDeliberation(packetSpecFolder, relativePath, content, options = {}) {
555:   const { aiCouncilRoot } = councilRootFor(packetSpecFolder);
556:   fs.mkdirSync(aiCouncilRoot, { recursive: true });
557:   rejectParentTraversal(relativePath, aiCouncilRoot);
558:   const deliberationPath = relativePath.startsWith('deliberations/')
559:     ? relativePath
560:     : path.join('deliberations', relativePath);
561:   return writeFileScoped(aiCouncilRoot, deliberationPath, content, options);
562: }
563: 
564: /**
565:  * Write a critique artifact under the council critiques directory.
566:  *
567:  * @param {string} packetSpecFolder - Packet spec folder to write into
568:  * @param {string} relativePath - Critique-relative or ai-council-relative path
569:  * @param {string} content - Critique markdown content
570:  * @param {Object} [options={}] - Scoped write and audit options
571:  * @returns {string} Absolute path to the written artifact
572:  */
573: function writeCritique(packetSpecFolder, relativePath, content, options = {}) {
574:   const { aiCouncilRoot } = councilRootFor(packetSpecFolder);
575:   fs.mkdirSync(aiCouncilRoot, { recursive: true });
576:   rejectParentTraversal(relativePath, aiCouncilRoot);
577:   const critiquePath = relativePath.startsWith('critiques/') ? relativePath : path.join('critiques', relativePath);
578:   return writeFileScoped(aiCouncilRoot, critiquePath, content, options);
579: }
580: 
581: /**
582:  * Write the final council report artifact.
583:  *
584:  * @param {string} packetSpecFolder - Packet spec folder to write into
585:  * @param {string} content - Council report markdown content
586:  * @param {Object} [options={}] - Scoped write and audit options
587:  * @returns {string} Absolute path to the written artifact
588:  */
589: function writeReport(packetSpecFolder, content, options = {}) {
590:   const { aiCouncilRoot } = councilRootFor(packetSpecFolder);
591:   fs.mkdirSync(aiCouncilRoot, { recursive: true });
592:   return writeFileScoped(aiCouncilRoot, 'council-report.md', content, options);
593: }
594: 
595: /**
596:  * Write all rendered council artifacts and collect conflict messages.
597:  *
598:  * @param {string} packetSpecFolder - Packet spec folder to write into
599:  * @param {Object} rendered - Rendered artifact payloads
600:  * @returns {Object} Written paths and conflict messages
601:  */
602: function writeArtifacts(packetSpecFolder, rendered) {
603:   const { packetRoot, aiCouncilRoot } = councilRootFor(packetSpecFolder);
604: 
605:   const written = [];
606:   const conflicts = [];
607:   fs.mkdirSync(packetRoot, { recursive: true });
608:   fs.mkdirSync(aiCouncilRoot, { recursive: true });
609: 
610:   const roundId = rendered.deliberationPath
611:     ? path.basename(rendered.deliberationPath, '.md')
612:     : 'round-001';
613:   const files = [
614:     () => writeStateJsonl(packetRoot, rendered.stateLog, { round_id: roundId, timestamp: DEFAULT_TIMESTAMP }),
615:     () => writeConfig(packetRoot, rendered.config, { round_id: roundId, timestamp: DEFAULT_TIMESTAMP }),
616:     () => writeStrategyMd(packetRoot, rendered.strategy, { round_id: roundId, timestamp: DEFAULT_TIMESTAMP }),
617:     () => writeDeliberation(packetRoot, rendered.deliberationPath || 'deliberations/round-001.md', rendered.deliberation, { round_id: roundId, timestamp: DEFAULT_TIMESTAMP }),
618:     () => writeReport(packetRoot, rendered.councilReport, { round_id: roundId, timestamp: DEFAULT_TIMESTAMP }),
619:     ...rendered.seats.map((seat) => () => writeSeat(packetRoot, seat.path, seat.content, {
620:       round_id: roundId,
621:       seat_id: path.basename(seat.path).replace(/\.md$/, '').split('-').slice(0, 2).join('-'),
622:       timestamp: DEFAULT_TIMESTAMP,
623:     })),
624:   ];
625: 
626:   for (const writeFile of files) {
627:     try {
628:       written.push(writeFile());
629:     } catch (error) {
630:       conflicts.push(error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error));
631:     }
632:   }
633: 
634:   return { written, conflicts };
635: }
636: 
637: /**
638:  * Append one normalized event to the packet council state log.
639:  *
640:  * @param {string} packetSpecFolder - Packet spec folder containing ai-council
641:  * @param {Object} event - State event payload
642:  * @returns {string} Absolute path to the state JSONL file
643:  */
644: function appendStateLine(packetSpecFolder, event) {
645:   const { aiCouncilRoot } = councilRootFor(packetSpecFolder);
646:   const statePath = path.resolve(aiCouncilRoot, 'ai-council-state.jsonl');
647:   assertInside(aiCouncilRoot, statePath);
648:   fs.mkdirSync(aiCouncilRoot, { recursive: true });
649:   fs.appendFileSync(statePath, `${JSON.stringify(metadataEvent(event))}\n`, 'utf8');
650:   return statePath;
651: }
652: 
653: function parseArgs(argv) {
654:   const args = {
655:     packetSpecFolder: null,
656:     round: 1,
657:     inputFile: null,
658:     strictOutput: false,
659:     force: false,
660:     memorySavePayloadOut: null,
661:   };
662:   const rest = [...argv];
663:   args.packetSpecFolder = rest.shift() || null;
664:   while (rest.length) {
665:     const flag = rest.shift();
666:     if (flag === '--round') args.round = Number(rest.shift());
667:     else if (flag === '--input-file') args.inputFile = rest.shift();
668:     else if (flag === '--memory-save-payload-out') args.memorySavePayloadOut = rest.shift();
669:     else if (flag === '--strict-output') args.strictOutput = true;
670:     else if (flag === '--force') args.force = true;
671:     else throw new Error(`[ai-council] Unknown argument: ${flag}`);
672:   }
673:   if (!args.packetSpecFolder) {
674:     throw new Error('Usage: node persist-artifacts.cjs <packet-spec-folder> [--round NNN] [--input-file FILE] [--memory-save-payload-out FILE] [--strict-output] [--force]');
675:   }
676:   if (args.memorySavePayloadOut === undefined) {
677:     throw new Error('[ai-council] --memory-save-payload-out requires a file path');
678:   }
679:   roundLabel(args.round);
680:   return args;
681: }
682: 
683: function readInput(inputFile) {
684:   if (inputFile) return fs.readFileSync(inputFile, 'utf8');
685:   return fs.readFileSync(0, 'utf8');
686: }
687: 
688: /**
689:  * Run the council artifact persistence CLI.
690:  *
691:  * @param {string[]} [argv=process.argv.slice(2)] - CLI arguments
692:  * @returns {number} Process exit code
693:  */
694: function main(argv = process.argv.slice(2)) {
695:   let args;
696:   try {
697:     args = parseArgs(argv);
698:     const markdown = readInput(args.inputFile);
699:     if (!normalizeText(markdown)) throw new Error('[ai-council] No council report input provided');
700:     const parsed = parseCouncilReport(markdown);
701:     if (!parsed.ok) {
702:       console.error(`[ai-council] Missing required section(s): ${parsed.missing.join(', ')}`);
703:       return 1;
704:     }
705:     const rendered = renderArtifacts(parsed, {
706:       round: args.round,
707:       strictOutput: args.strictOutput,
708:       specFolder: path.resolve(args.packetSpecFolder),
709:     });
710:     const result = writeArtifacts(args.packetSpecFolder, rendered, { force: args.force });
711:     if (result.conflicts.length) {
712:       console.error(`[ai-council] Partial write failure:\n${result.conflicts.join('\n')}`);
713:       return 2;
714:     }
715:     if (args.memorySavePayloadOut) {
716:       try {
717:         const payload = buildMemorySavePayload(parsed, args.packetSpecFolder);
718:         fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(path.resolve(args.memorySavePayloadOut)), { recursive: true });
719:         fs.writeFileSync(path.resolve(args.memorySavePayloadOut), `${JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2)}\n`, 'utf8');
720:       } catch (payloadError) {
721:         console.warn(`[ai-council] Failed to write memory-save payload: ${payloadError instanceof Error ? payloadError.message : String(payloadError)}`);
722:         return 2;
723:       }
724:     }
725:     console.log(`[ai-council] Wrote ${result.written.length} artifact(s) to ${path.resolve(args.packetSpecFolder, 'ai-council')}`);
726:     return 0;
727:   } catch (error) {
728:     console.error(error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error));
729:     return 1;
730:   }
731: }
732: 
733: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
734: // 5. EXPORTS
735: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
736: 
737: module.exports = {
738:   SCHEMA_VERSION,
739:   PROTOCOL,
740:   PRODUCER_VERSION,
741:   parseCouncilReport,
742:   parseStateLine,
743:   parseStateLog,
744:   renderArtifacts,
745:   buildMemorySavePayload,
746:   writeConfig,
747:   writeStrategyMd,
748:   writeStateJsonl,
749:   writeSeat,
750:   writeDeliberation,
751:   writeCritique,
752:   writeReport,
753:   writeArtifacts,
754:   appendStateLine,
755:   main,
756: };
757: 
758: if (require.main === module) {
759:   process.exitCode = main();
760: }

<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: // ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
3: // ║ loop-host — mode-switching entry point for deep-improvement runs         ║
4: // ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
5: 'use strict';
6: 
7: /**
8:  * Mode-switching entry point for deep-improvement. Routes between the
9:  * existing `agent-improvement` scoring path and the new `model-benchmark`
10:  * pipeline. This is the seam host that was previously missing
11:  * (deep-improvement has no loop.cjs — orchestration is journal-based).
12:  *
13:  * Backward-compat contract: with `--mode=agent-improvement` OR no
14:  * `--mode` flag, behavior is identical — both route to score-candidate.cjs with
15:  * the same arguments, so the produced score/state output is byte-identical.
16:  *
17:  * Usage:
18:  *   agent-improvement (default):
19:  *     node loop-host.cjs [--mode=agent-improvement] --candidate=<path> [--baseline=<path>] [--output=<path>]
20:  *   model-benchmark:
21:  *     node loop-host.cjs --mode=model-benchmark --profile=<path-or-id> --outputs-dir=<path> \
22:  *        [--output=<path>] [--state-log=<path>] [--label=<string>] [--profiles-dir=<path>] \
23:  *        [--scorer=<pattern|5dim>] [--grader=<noop|mock|llm>]
24:  *   non-dev-ai-system-refine (Lane D):
25:  *     node loop-host.cjs --mode=non-dev-ai-system-refine --packaging-root=<path> \
26:  *        [--live] [--max-iters=<n>] [--fixtures=<a,b>] [--variants=<a,b>] \
27:  *        [--held-out=<a,b>] [--samples=<n>] [--proposer-model=<id>] [--grader-model=<id>]
28:  *
29:  * Unknown --mode values warn to stderr and fall back to agent-improvement.
30:  */
31: 
32: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
33: // 1. IMPORTS
34: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
35: 
36: const path = require('path');
37: const { spawnSync } = require('child_process');
38: const { parseArgs } = require('./parse-args.cjs');
39: 
40: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
41: // 2. CONSTANTS
42: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
43: 
44: const SCRIPTS_ROOT = __dirname;
45: const VALID_MODES = new Set(['agent-improvement', 'model-benchmark', 'skill-benchmark', 'non-dev-ai-system-refine']);
46: 
47: // Lane separation: planInvocation() returns BARE script names (e.g.
48: // 'score-candidate.cjs') to keep the backward-compat identity plan
49: // byte-identical. The real on-disk lane lives in sibling lane dirs relative to
50: // this file (now scripts/shared/). resolveScriptPath maps a bare name to its
51: // lane path at SPAWN time so the plan output never carries lane segments.
52: const LANE_A = new Set([
53:   'score-candidate.cjs',
54:   'generate-profile.cjs',
55:   'rollback-candidate.cjs',
56:   'candidate-lineage.cjs',
57:   'scan-integration.cjs',
58:   'check-mirror-drift.cjs',
59:   'trade-off-detector.cjs',
60:   'benchmark-stability.cjs',
61: ]);
62: const LANE_MODEL_BENCHMARK = new Set([
63:   'run-benchmark.cjs',
64:   'dispatch-model.cjs',
65: ]);
66: // Lane C (skill-benchmark): the orchestrator the run resolves to. Sub-scripts
67: // (router-replay, contamination-lint, d5-connectivity, parse-resource-loads,
68: // score-skill-benchmark, build-report) are required internally by the
69: // orchestrator, so only the entry script needs lane-path resolution here.
70: const LANE_SKILL_BENCHMARK = new Set([
71:   'run-skill-benchmark.cjs',
72: ]);
73: // Lane D (non-dev-ai-system-refine): a thin adapter that spawns the loop
74: // host living WITH the packaging under test (<packaging-root>/benchmark/_loop/loop.py).
75: // The guarded loop logic (frozen scoring surface, independent re-grade,
76: // worktree promote-N, kill-switches) belongs to the packaging, not this skill.
77: const LANE_NON_DEV_AI_SYSTEM = new Set([
78:   'run-non-dev-ai-system.cjs',
79: ]);
80: 
81: // Optional flags loop-host forwards to run-non-dev-ai-system.cjs, in forwarding
82: // order. --packaging-root is required and handled separately. This list is
83: // exactly the surface run-non-dev-ai-system.cjs consumes (--live + its
84: // ENV_FORWARD map + --max-iters).
85: const NON_DEV_AI_SYSTEM_RUN_OPTIONS = [
86:   'live',
87:   'max-iters',
88:   'fixtures',
89:   'variants',
90:   'held-out',
91:   'samples',
92:   'proposer-model',
93:   'grader-model',
94: ];
95: 
96: // Optional flags loop-host forwards to run-skill-benchmark.cjs, in forwarding
97: // order. --skill and --outputs-dir are required and handled separately. This
98: // list is exactly the set run-skill-benchmark.cjs's run() reads; forwarding a
99: // flag the orchestrator never consumes would be silently absorbed and mislead
100: // operators into thinking it has effect.
101: const SKILL_BENCHMARK_RUN_OPTIONS = [
102:   'fixtures-dir',
103:   'output',
104:   'trace-mode',
105:   'advisor-mode',
106:   'scenarios',
107:   'executor',
108:   'playbook-dir',
109: ];
110: 
111: // Single source of truth for the optional model-benchmark flags loop-host
112: // forwards to run-benchmark.cjs (one shared benchmark option schema). Order is
113: // the forwarding order. --profile and --outputs-dir are required and handled
114: // separately. Keep this aligned with run-benchmark.cjs's accepted options.
115: const BENCHMARK_RUN_OPTIONS = [
116:   'output',
117:   'state-log',
118:   'label',
119:   'profiles-dir',
120:   'integration-report',
121:   'scorer',
122:   'grader',
123:   'samples',
124:   'allow-same-family',
125: ];
126: 
127: // The fixture materializer also needs --profiles-dir so a profile passed by ID
128: // (not a path) resolves identically in BOTH steps. --profile and
129: // --outputs-dir are forwarded explicitly; this lists the remaining
130: // materialize-relevant pass-through flags.
131: const BENCHMARK_MATERIALIZE_OPTIONS = [
132:   'profiles-dir',
133: ];
134: 
135: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
136: // 3. HELPERS
137: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
138: 
139: /**
140:  * Map a bare lane script name to its on-disk path at spawn time.
141:  *
142:  * @param {string} scriptName - Bare script name (e.g. 'score-candidate.cjs')
143:  * @returns {string} Absolute path to the resolved lane script
144:  */
145: function resolveScriptPath(scriptName) {
146:   if (LANE_A.has(scriptName)) {
147:     return path.join(SCRIPTS_ROOT, '..', 'agent-improvement', scriptName);
148:   }
149:   if (LANE_MODEL_BENCHMARK.has(scriptName)) {
150:     return path.join(SCRIPTS_ROOT, '..', 'model-benchmark', scriptName);
151:   }
152:   if (LANE_SKILL_BENCHMARK.has(scriptName)) {
153:     return path.join(SCRIPTS_ROOT, '..', 'skill-benchmark', scriptName);
154:   }
155:   if (LANE_NON_DEV_AI_SYSTEM.has(scriptName)) {
156:     return path.join(SCRIPTS_ROOT, '..', 'non-dev-ai-system', scriptName);
157:   }
158:   // Other shared scripts (materialize-benchmark-fixtures, promote-candidate,
159:   // reduce-state, improvement-journal, mutation-coverage) live alongside this file.
160:   return path.join(SCRIPTS_ROOT, scriptName);
161: }
162: 
163: /**
164:  * Resolve a raw --mode value to a valid mode, defaulting unknown values.
165:  *
166:  * @param {string|undefined} rawMode - Raw --mode flag value
167:  * @returns {string} A valid mode, falling back to 'agent-improvement'
168:  */
169: function resolveMode(rawMode) {
170:   if (rawMode === undefined) return 'agent-improvement';
171:   if (VALID_MODES.has(rawMode)) return rawMode;
172:   process.stderr.write(`loop-host: unknown mode '${rawMode}', defaulting to 'agent-improvement'\n`);
173:   return 'agent-improvement';
174: }
175: 
176: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
177: // 4. CORE LOGIC
178: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
179: 
180: /**
181:  * Pure planner: map (mode, args) -> the ordered script invocations to run.
182:  * Planning is separated from execution so the backward-compat identity gate
183:  * the identity gate can assert byte-identical plans for the default and explicit
184:  * agent-improvement routes without spawning anything.
185:  *
186:  * @param {string} mode - Resolved run mode (agent-improvement, model-benchmark, skill-benchmark, non-dev-ai-system-refine)
187:  * @param {object} args - Parsed CLI args keyed by flag name
188:  * @returns {{ ok: true, steps: Array<{script: string, args: string[]}> } | { ok: false, error: string }}
189:  */
190: function planInvocation(mode, args) {
191:   if (mode === 'model-benchmark') {
192:     if (!args.profile || !args['outputs-dir']) {
193:       return { ok: false, error: 'model-benchmark: missing required --profile=<path-or-id> and --outputs-dir=<path>' };
194:     }
195:     // materialize MUST run (and succeed) before run-benchmark, else scoring
196:     // silently produces all-zero "missing-output" results. These scripts use
197:     // space-separated args.
198:     //
199:     // Both steps share one option schema (BENCHMARK_* lists) so forwarding stays
200:     // in sync with run-benchmark.cjs and the materializer, instead of a hand-rolled
201:     // per-flag chain that silently drifts.
202:     // run-benchmark.cjs forwards scorer/grader/integration-report so the journal
203:     // and report.json agree on the requested method; the materializer forwards
204:     // --profiles-dir so a profile-by-ID resolves identically in both steps.
205:     const materializeArgs = ['--profile', args.profile, '--outputs-dir', args['outputs-dir']];
206:     for (const opt of BENCHMARK_MATERIALIZE_OPTIONS) {
207:       if (args[opt] !== undefined) materializeArgs.push(`--${opt}`, String(args[opt]));
208:     }
209:     const benchArgs = ['--profile', args.profile, '--outputs-dir', args['outputs-dir']];
210:     for (const opt of BENCHMARK_RUN_OPTIONS) {
211:       if (args[opt] !== undefined) benchArgs.push(`--${opt}`, String(args[opt]));
212:     }
213:     return {
214:       ok: true,
215:       steps: [
216:         { script: 'materialize-benchmark-fixtures.cjs', args: materializeArgs },
217:         { script: 'run-benchmark.cjs', args: benchArgs },
218:       ],
219:     };
220:   }
221:   if (mode === 'skill-benchmark') {
222:     if (!args.skill || !args['outputs-dir']) {
223:       return { ok: false, error: 'skill-benchmark: missing required --skill=<skill-root-or-id> and --outputs-dir=<path>' };
224:     }
225:     // Single orchestrator step: run-skill-benchmark.cjs internally sequences
226:     // D5-gate -> router-replay -> (optional live) -> trace-parse -> score ->
227:     // report, so the plan stays one step (unlike Lane B's materialize+run pair).
228:     // Lane C scripts use space-separated args.
229:     const skillArgs = ['--skill', args.skill, '--outputs-dir', args['outputs-dir']];
230:     for (const opt of SKILL_BENCHMARK_RUN_OPTIONS) {
231:       if (args[opt] !== undefined) skillArgs.push(`--${opt}`, String(args[opt]));
232:     }
233:     return { ok: true, steps: [{ script: 'run-skill-benchmark.cjs', args: skillArgs }] };
234:   }
235:   if (mode === 'non-dev-ai-system-refine') {
236:     if (!args['packaging-root']) {
237:       return { ok: false, error: 'non-dev-ai-system-refine: missing required --packaging-root=<path>' };
238:     }
239:     // Single adapter step (same single-orchestrator plan shape as Lane C): run-non-dev-ai-system.cjs validates the
240:     // benchmark/_loop/loop.py contract and spawns the packaging's own guarded loop host.
241:     // Lane D scripts use space-separated args. --live is a boolean: forwarded
242:     // bare so the adapter's parser keeps it a flag, not a key/value pair.
243:     const refineArgs = ['--packaging-root', String(args['packaging-root'])];
244:     for (const opt of NON_DEV_AI_SYSTEM_RUN_OPTIONS) {
245:       if (args[opt] === undefined) continue;
246:       if (args[opt] === true) refineArgs.push(`--${opt}`);
247:       else refineArgs.push(`--${opt}`, String(args[opt]));
248:     }
249:     return { ok: true, steps: [{ script: 'run-non-dev-ai-system.cjs', args: refineArgs }] };
250:   }
251:   // agent-improvement (default). score-candidate.cjs uses key=value args.
252:   if (!args.candidate) {
253:     return { ok: false, error: 'agent-improvement: missing required --candidate=<path>' };
254:   }
255:   const scoreArgs = [`--candidate=${args.candidate}`];
256:   if (args.baseline) scoreArgs.push(`--baseline=${args.baseline}`);
257:   if (args.output) scoreArgs.push(`--output=${args.output}`);
258:   return { ok: true, steps: [{ script: 'score-candidate.cjs', args: scoreArgs }] };
259: }
260: 
261: function runNode(scriptName, scriptArgs) {
262:   const scriptPath = resolveScriptPath(scriptName);
263:   const res = spawnSync('node', [scriptPath, ...scriptArgs], {
264:     stdio: 'inherit',
265:     encoding: 'utf8',
266:   });
267:   return res.status == null ? 1 : res.status;
268: }
269: 
270: function runPlan(plan) {
271:   if (!plan.ok) {
272:     process.stderr.write(`loop-host: ${plan.error}\n`);
273:     return 2;
274:   }
275:   for (const step of plan.steps) {
276:     const exit = runNode(step.script, step.args);
277:     if (exit !== 0) {
278:       process.stderr.write(`loop-host: ${step.script} failed (exit ${exit}); aborting remaining steps\n`);
279:       return exit;
280:     }
281:   }
282:   return 0;
283: }
284: 
285: function main() {
286:   const args = parseArgs(process.argv.slice(2));
287:   const mode = resolveMode(args.mode);
288:   process.exit(runPlan(planInvocation(mode, args)));
289: }
290: 
291: if (require.main === module) main();
292: 
293: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
294: // 5. EXPORTS
295: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
296: 
297: module.exports = { parseArgs, resolveMode, planInvocation, resolveScriptPath, VALID_MODES, LANE_SKILL_BENCHMARK, LANE_NON_DEV_AI_SYSTEM };

(End of file - total 297 lines)

2: // ║ promote-candidate — guarded canonical promotion helper                   ║
3: // ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
4: 'use strict';
5: 
6: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
7: // 1. IMPORTS
8: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
9: 
10: const fs = require('node:fs');
11: const path = require('node:path');
12: const {
13:   BENCHMARK_AGGREGATE_GATE,
14:   PROMOTION_GATES,
15:   WEIGHTED_SCORE_GATE,
16:   evaluateMirrorSyncGate,
17:   evaluatePromotionGates,

20:   inferAgentNameFromPath,

24: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
25: // 2. HELPERS
26: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
27: 
28: function parseArgs(argv) {
29:   const args = {};
30:   for (const entry of argv) {
31:     if (!entry.startsWith('--')) {
32:       continue;
33:     }
34:     const [key, ...rest] = entry.slice(2).split('=');
35:     args[key] = rest.length > 0 ? rest.join('=') : true;
36:   }
37:   return args;

40: function readJson(filePath) {
41:   return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8'));
42: }
43: 
44: function readOptionalJson(filePath) {
45:   if (!filePath || !fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
46:     return null;
47:   }
48:   return readJson(filePath);

51: function readJsonc(filePath) {
52:   const raw = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8');
53:   return JSON.parse(raw.replace(/^\s*\/\/.*$/gm, ''));
54: }
55: 
56: function ensureParent(filePath) {
57:   fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(filePath), { recursive: true });
58: }
59: 
60: function writeJson(filePath, data) {
61:   ensureParent(filePath);
62:   fs.writeFileSync(filePath, `${JSON.stringify(data, null, 2)}\n`, 'utf8');
63: }
64: 
65: function appendJsonl(filePath, data) {
66:   ensureParent(filePath);
67:   fs.appendFileSync(filePath, `${JSON.stringify(data)}\n`, 'utf8');
68: }
69: 
70: // Dual-shape contract (deep-review R1-P1-002, reconciled): current producers always emit the
71: // primitive number shape; the object shape ({value}) is legacy-tolerated. New score producers
72: // MUST emit a number.
73: function readScoreDelta(score) {
74:   if (score && typeof score.delta === 'object' && score.delta !== null) {
75:     return score.delta.total;
76:   }
77:   return score ? score.delta : null;
78: }

80: function isAgentDefinitionTarget(target) {
81:   const normalized = (path.isAbsolute(target) ? path.relative(process.cwd(), target) : target)
82:     .split(path.sep)
83:     .join('/');
84:   return /^(\.\/)?\.(opencode|claude)\/agents\/[^/]+\.md$/.test(normalized)
85:     || /^(\.\/)?\.codex\/agents\/[^/]+\.toml$/.test(normalized);

87: 
88: function expectedFormatForTarget(target) {
89:   const normalized = (path.isAbsolute(target) ? path.relative(process.cwd(), target) : target)
90:     .split(path.sep)
91:     .join('/');
92:   return normalized.includes('.codex/agents/') ? 'codex-toml' : 'markdown';

95: function writeMirrorSyncState(stateFilePath, state) {
96:   if (!stateFilePath) {
97:     return;
98:   }
99:   const payload = {
100:     type: 'mirror_sync_state',
101:     timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),

111: function rejectWithStructuredError(errorType, message, details, stateFilePath) {
112:   if (details?.mirror_sync_state) {

115:   process.stderr.write(`${JSON.stringify({
116:     status: 'error',

124: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
125: // 3. VALIDATION CHECKS

128: function resolveAllowedCanonicalTarget(manifestPath) {
129:   const manifest = readJsonc(manifestPath);
130:   const canonicalTargets = (manifest.targets || [])
131:     .filter((target) => target.classification === 'canonical')
132:     .map((target) => target.path);

135:     process.exit(1);

140: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

144: function main() {
145:   const args = parseArgs(process.argv.slice(2));
146:   const candidate = args.candidate;

154:   const approve = args.approve === true || args.approve === 'true';
155: 
156:   // Lane B (model-benchmark) produces report.json with
157:   // status=benchmark-complete and never a scored agent file. Promotion mode is
158:   // therefore selected by the presence of --score: when --score is supplied this
159:   // is the Lane A agent path (scored candidate + agent dimension gates, byte-
160:   // behavior unchanged); when --score is omitted and --benchmark-report points at
161:   // a benchmark-complete + benchmark-pass report this is the Lane B benchmark path,

163:   // requirement and agent dimension gates. The shared guards (config promotion
164:   // flags, benchmark/repeatability gates, single canonical target, and agent-
165:   // definition mirror sync) apply to both modes.

168:   if (!candidate || !target || !benchmarkReportPath || !configPath || !manifestPath || !archiveDir || !approve) {
169:     process.stderr.write('Usage (Lane A / agent): node promote-candidate.cjs --candidate=... --target=... --score=... --benchmark-report=... [--repeatability-report=...] --config=... --manifest=... --archive-dir=... --approve\n');

250:   // Spec 143 T2 (rubric guard): the optimizer must never write its own ruler.

263:         { baselineRegions: verdict.baselineRegions, candidateRegions: verdict.candidateRegions },

271:     process.exit(1);

277:   // benchmark report gates verified above (benchmark-complete + benchmark-pass +
278:   // aggregate gate + repeatability) and skips these agent-only checks.
279:   let scoreDelta = null;

303: 
304:   let runtimeMirrorSync = null;

309:       repoRoot: process.cwd(),

315:     if (!mirrorGate.passed) {
316:       rejectWithStructuredError(
317:         'MIRROR_SYNC_GATE_FAILED',
318:         'Cannot promote: 4-runtime agent mirror sync verification failed',
319:         {

322:           agentName,
323:           mirror_sync_state: mirrorGate.mirror_sync_state,
324:           recoveryAction: mirrorGate.recoveryAction,
325:           defaultRecovery: 'rollback partial mirrors before resume to maintain 4-runtime invariant',
326:           presentRuntimes: syncResult.presentRuntimes,
327:           missingRuntimes: syncResult.missingRuntimes,
328:           driftRuntimes: syncResult.driftRuntimes,
329:           verification: syncResult,
330:         },
331:         mirrorStateFilePath
332:       );
333:     }
334: 
335:     writeMirrorSyncState(mirrorStateFilePath, {
336:       target,
337:       candidate,
338:       agentName,
339:       mirror_sync_state: mirrorGate.mirror_sync_state,
340:       recoveryAction: mirrorGate.recoveryAction,
341:       defaultRecovery: null,
342:       verification: syncResult,
343:     });
344:   }
345: 
346:   ensureParent(path.join(archiveDir, 'placeholder'));
347:   const timestamp = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[:]/g, '-');
348:   const backupPath = path.join(archiveDir, `${path.basename(target)}.${timestamp}.bak`);

351:   fs.copyFileSync(candidate, target);
352: 
353:   // Lane A output shape is unchanged (byte-behavior
354:   // contract). Lane B emits mode=benchmark plus the benchmark aggregate/delta so
355:   // the promotion result is self-describing for the model-benchmark loop.
356:   const result = benchmarkMode
357:     ? {
358:         status: 'promoted',
359:         mode: 'benchmark',
360:         target,
361:         candidate,
362:         backupPath,
363:         benchmarkReport: benchmarkReportPath,
364:         repeatabilityReport: resolvedRepeatabilityReportPath,
365:         runtimeMirrors: runtimeMirrorSync,
366:         mirror_sync_state: runtimeMirrorSync ? 'all_landed' : null,
367:         aggregateScore: benchmarkReport.aggregateScore,
368:         benchmarkDelta: benchmarkReport.totals?.delta ?? null,
369:         recommendation: benchmarkReport.recommendation,
370:         timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
371:       }
372:     : {
373:         status: 'promoted',
374:         target,
375:         candidate,
376:         backupPath,
377:         benchmarkReport: benchmarkReportPath,
378:         repeatabilityReport: resolvedRepeatabilityReportPath,
379:         runtimeMirrors: runtimeMirrorSync,
380:         mirror_sync_state: runtimeMirrorSync ? 'all_landed' : null,
381:         delta: scoreDelta,
382:         threshold,
383:         timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
384:       };
385: 
386:   process.stdout.write(`${JSON.stringify(result, null, 2)}\n`);
387: }
388: 
389: main();
